title: Pensions and Fertility: Microeconomic Evidence
population: Women of fertile age in rural-worker pension expansion context
treatment_or_exposure: Expansion and increased generosity of rural-worker pensions
primary_outcome_measure: Newborn child (under one year old); Completed fertility
identification_source: Rural pension system expansion and pension wealth shock
external_validity_rationale: Strong mechanism setting for family old-age support but rural Brazil pension expansion may overstate transportability to mature high-income SDT welfare states.
fulltext_reason: Direct old-age-security pension generosity shock with fertility outcomes

title: The Old-Age Security Motive for Fertility: Evidence from the Extension of Social Pensions in Namibia
population: Women exposed to regional and cohort variation in social pension extension
treatment_or_exposure: Extension of social pensions across regions and cohorts
identification_source: Pre-reform pension differences and exposure to pension equalization
external_validity_rationale: Highly direct mechanism evidence for settings with family old-age support but less directly transportable to mature OECD SDT settings.
fulltext_reason: Direct old-age-security pension expansion with fertility outcomes

title: What Explains Fertility? Evidence from Italian Pension Reforms
population: Workers affected by Italian pension reforms of the 1990s
treatment_or_exposure: Less generous future pensions from Italian pension reforms
primary_outcome_measure: number of children born starting from the year after each of the reforms; probability of having an additional child
design: Natural experiment / discontinuity by worker treatment status
external_validity_rationale: High-income European pension reform is more transportable to SDT settings than rural or historical studies though treatment is generosity expectations rather than new pension introduction.
fulltext_reason: Direct pension generosity and fertility test

title: The impact of long-term care insurance on family fertility behaviour: evidence from China
authors: Yuqing Han; Xuhui Tao; Siyuan Wang; Yi Zhang
population: Families in China Family Panel Studies exposed to LTCI pilot rollout
primary_outcome_measure: Have you given birth in the past year?; How many children do you expect to have?
identification_source: Pilot rollout of long-term care insurance
external_validity_rationale: Fertility outcome and social-insurance treatment are relevant but the margin is care-risk insurance rather than pension income; sign may vary by insured family member age.
fulltext_reason: Fertility outcome with elder-care insurance mechanism

title: The Introduction of Bismarck's Social Security System and its Effects on Marriage and Fertility in Prussia
population: Prussian populations exposed to Bismarck social insurance pillars
treatment_or_exposure: Health accident and old-age insurance under Bismarck's social security system
identification_source: Timing and regional/professional exposure to social insurance pillars
kinship_system_or_son_preference: marriage channel and historical family support norms
external_validity_rationale: Historical European social-insurance setting maps closely to the FDT but not to SDT welfare-state contexts.
fulltext_reason: Historical social-insurance treatment with marriage and fertility outcomes

title: The fertility effects of public pension: Evidence from the new rural pension scheme in China
authors: Zheng Shen; Xiaodong Zheng; Hualei Yang
primary_outcome_measure: number of children; having a second child
kinship_system_or_son_preference: son preference and rural family support may be relevant
external_validity_rationale: Direct pension/fertility evidence in a rural China setting with high family-support relevance; transportability to low-fertility high-income SDT is limited.
fulltext_reason: Direct rural pension expansion with fertility outcomes

title: Children as insurance revisited: Impact of children on private insurance adoption among older parents
treatment_or_exposure: Number of children instrumented by the Later Longer Fewer campaign
primary_outcome_measure: Have private insurance; private pension; private health insurance
identification_source: Later Longer Fewer campaign as fertility instrument
kinship_system_or_son_preference: children-as-insurance mechanism and older-parent risk management
external_validity_rationale: Mechanism-relevant but fertility is the treatment and private insurance is the outcome; useful for theory/mechanism narrative rather than OAS-to-fertility effect synthesis.

title: Pensions and fertility: back to the roots
population: Provinces of Imperial Germany exposed to Bismarck pension-system variation
identification_source: Regional variation across 23 provinces in key pension-system variables
external_validity_rationale: Directly targets the first demographic transition and historical pension introduction; weak transportability to SDT because context predates mature welfare states.
fulltext_reason: Historical Bismarck pension exposure with fertility outcomes

title: Fertility and Financial Development: Evidence from U.S. Counties in the 19th Century
authors: Alberto Basso; Howard Bodenhorn; David Cuberes
treatment_or_exposure: Financial-market development as substitute old-age security
identification_source: Presence of banks and local financial development
kinship_system_or_son_preference: pre-modern and transition-era family support norms
external_validity_rationale: Strong mechanism relevance for pre-welfare settings but treatment is financial-market access rather than pension or social insurance.
fulltext_reason: Indirect old-age-security test through financial development

title: The US baby boom and the 1935 Social Security Act
population: U.S. women and state-level fertility by parity
treatment_or_exposure: State social spending under the 1935 Social Security Act
primary_outcome_measure: total fertility rate (births adjusted by the female population between 14 and 44 years and per thousand); number of own children under the age of five; number of children ever born
identification_source: Cross-state variation in allowed social spending under SSA
data_source: Individual census data and state-level fertility by parity
external_validity_rationale: Fertility outcome is useful but the treatment combines family allowances and broader social spending; not a clean old-age-security pension estimate.
fulltext_reason: Social Security Act fertility evidence but mechanism is broader than OAS
